prompt: electric creature-battle title letters with arcade speed, comet-fang weirdness, chunky sticker energy, sharp readable monster-showdown drama Codex learning constraints: Fused-engine diagnosis: clean counters helped, but generic athletic chamfers are not enough. Add oversized creature-specific events: jaw apertures, fang terminals, broken shoulders, hooked counters, and voltage bridges that remain visible at mobile proof size. Prompt patches: Next pass: judge only the undecorated black proof first. Keep the reverse-slanted monster-title stance, but replace repeated esports chamfer chips with one oversized electric-fang structural event per uppercase and numeral. Make C an open jaw with a clear lower fang, G a jaw with an unmistakable inward voltage hook, O a closed charged ring with one large asymmetric break, 0 a slashed form clearly unlike O, 6 an open top hook with lower bite, 8 two pinched counters, and 9 a top loop with a descending fang tail. Apply mint fill, heavy black sticker outline, and purple shadow only after the raw silhouettes pass. Mobile hero: larger centered two-line VOLTAGE / FANG with no crop and less dead space. Next pass: judge only the undecorated black proof first. Keep the reverse-slanted monster-title stance, but remove repeated small chips as the main texture. Each uppercase and numeral needs one large structural electric-fang event visible on mobile. Prioritize C=open jaw with lower fang, G=jaw plus oversized inward voltage hook, O=closed ring with one major voltage break, 0=clear slash unlike O, 6=open top hook plus lower bite, 8=two visibly pinched counters, 9=top loop with descending fang tail. Apply mint fill, black sticker outline, and purple shadow only after the raw silhouettes pass. Lessons: Do not approve Voltage Fang when the decorated hero carries more concept than the raw alphabet. Approval requires mobile-visible, individualized electric-fang silhouettes, especially in C/G/O and 0/6/8/9, plus deliberate mobile scale and tighter proof spacing. Do not approve Voltage Fang when the decorated hero carries the concept better than the raw alphabet. Approval requires visibly individualized electric-fang silhouettes in the black proof, especially C/G/O and 0/6/8/9. Do not approve Voltage Fang while the decorated hero is stronger than the raw alphabet or while mobile crops the title. Approval requires complete mobile composi...
sampleText:
VOLTAGE FANG
THE QUICK
BROWN TOOL
FANGS
VOLTAGE
& ODD
GLYPHS!
HAMBURGE
FONTSIV
0123456789

glyphConstruction.0: slashed zero with a different counter rhythm than O
glyphConstruction.1: offset flag and grounded base notch
glyphConstruction.2: top bite that turns into a fast diagonal ramp
glyphConstruction.3: stacked jaws with a hard center shelf
glyphConstruction.4: open triangular frame with a sharp cross-stab
glyphConstruction.5: clipped top shelf and lower bite
glyphConstruction.6: open top hook with a distinct lower loop bite
glyphConstruction.7: long falling lightning slash with one midair nick
glyphConstruction.8: two counters pinched by an offset center voltage seam
glyphConstruction.9: top loop and descending fang tail
glyphConstruction.A: split peak fang with a dropped voltage crossbar
glyphConstruction.B: two separate bowl bites so the upper and lower lobes do different jobs
glyphConstruction.C: open creature jaw with a lower fang spur
glyphConstruction.D: heavy back stem with one dorsal lightning bite
glyphConstruction.E: staggered electric arms with a broken middle shoulder
glyphConstruction.F: top-heavy arcade flag with a missing lower tooth
glyphConstruction.G: C jaw plus an inward voltage hook
glyphConstruction.H: paired pillars joined by a cracked lightning bridge
glyphConstruction.I: narrow charged spine with oversized caps
glyphConstruction.J: hooked tail that turns into a fang
glyphConstruction.K: broken knee where the diagonals collide
glyphConstruction.L: grounded foot with a forward bite
glyphConstruction.N: single bolt diagonal cutting across two blunt stems
glyphConstruction.O: closed charged ring with one major asymmetric voltage break
glyphConstruction.P: upper bowl punch with a quiet lower stem
glyphConstruction.Q: O ring plus a long comet tail
glyphConstruction.R: punched bowl and aggressive kicked leg
glyphConstruction.S: serpentine center with two opposing speed cuts
glyphConstruction.T: oversized top rail and narrow charged stem
glyphConstruction.U: bucket bottom with a bitten right shoulder
glyphConstruction.W: double fang rhythm with uneven outer claws
glyphConstruction.X: crossing voltage shards with a central collision chip
glyphConstruction.Y: forked upper arms feeding one blunt stem
glyphConstruction.Z: lightning rail with a snapped lower exit
